title: eryngium tricuspidatum subsp. mauritanicum
intro: Eryngium tricuspidatum subsp. mauritanicum is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the Mauritanian eryngium or sea holly. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Eryngium tricuspidatum subsp. mauritanicum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate respiratory issues.
